label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Wait, February usually ends on the 28th, except in a leap year when it has 29 days. Since 2000 is a leap year (because it's divisible by 400), the last day of February would be February 29th, right? So Jane's birthday is on 02/29/2000. Now, today is her 16-year-old birthday. That means today's date should be her birthday in 2016. But wait, 2016 is also a leap year (divisible by 4, and not a century year, so it is a leap year). So her birthday in 2016 would be February 29, 2016. So today is 02/29/2016. The question is asking for the date one week from today. Adding one week (7 days) to February 29, 2016. Let me count. February has 29 days in 2016. So if today is the 29th, adding 7 days would go beyond February. Let me see: February 29 + 1 day = March 1 Then adding 6 more days: March 1 + 6 days = March 7. Wait, because from the 1st to the 7th is 6 days later? Wait, if today is the 29th, adding 1 day is March 1 (that's 1 day from today). Then adding 6 more days would take us to March 7. So total 7 days. So one week from February 29 is March 7. Therefore, the date would be 03/07/2016. Looking at the options, option A is 03/07/2016. Let me check the options again: (A) 03/07/2016 (B) 02/23/2016 (C) 03/07/2070 (D) 03/06/2016 (E) 03/08/2016 So answer A seems correct. Wait, but maybe I made a mistake in counting? Let me check again. Starting from Feb 29, adding days: Day 1: March 1 (1 day later) Day 2: March 2 Day 3: March 3 Day 4: March 4 Day 5: March 5 Day 6: March 6 Day 7: March 7 Yes, exactly. So one week later is March 7. So the answer is A. But let me check if there's any possibility of a mistake in the reasoning. Another way to think: If today is her 16th birthday, that would be 16 years after 2000, which is 2016. Since she was born on February 29, which only comes every four years, on non-leap years her birthday might be considered March 1, but the problem says "today is her 16-year-old birthday".
